title: Workshop investment decision package
executiveSummary: Demo / Hypothesis - Workshop remains attractive, but decision confidence depends on interviews, WTP validation, and competitor penetration checks.
summary: A structured v2 research package covering decision metrics, unknowns, kill criteria, coverage, and next actions.

claims.statement: Workshop willingness to pay is the highest-impact unknown before investment-ready status.
claims.notes: Demo / Hypothesis - not a factual claim.

updatedScores.reason: Demo / Hypothesis - payment-flow score should wait for WTP validation.

criticalUnknowns.title: Willingness to pay PHP 1,500 per month
criticalUnknowns.description: Demo / Hypothesis - stated WTP is not yet validated.
criticalUnknowns.impactIfWrong: Revenue model may not support a vertical SaaS product.

killCriteria.title: Average willingness to pay is below PHP 500 per month
killCriteria.description: Demo / Hypothesis - low WTP would pause the thesis.

whyWeWin.title: Local workflow focus
whyWeWin.description: Demo / Hypothesis - a narrow workshop operating system can fit Messenger-led daily behavior better than broad tools.

whyWeMayLose.title: Inventory complexity
whyWeMayLose.description: Demo / Hypothesis - parts and oil inventory may pull the product into heavy scope.

coverageUpdates.gaps: Demo / Hypothesis - no completed WTP interview batch yet.

nextResearchActions.title: Interview 10 independent workshops
nextResearchActions.description: Demo / Hypothesis - validate workflow frequency, price sensitivity, and current workarounds.
nextResearchActions.reason: WTP is the largest decision-score gap.

decisionLogEntries.title: Prioritize WTP validation
decisionLogEntries.decision: Make WTP interviews the next critical research action.
decisionLogEntries.rationale: Demo / Hypothesis - Decision Score is most sensitive to payment willingness.
